mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness signals constrained wage growth and strong consumer spending capacity, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and residential investment, which drives broad-based equity outperformance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 55% of rolling 12-month windows, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects supply chain normalization and rising capacity utilization, which directly improves corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods consistently produce negative or flat 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers market repricing of future Federal Reserve policy, compressing equity valuation multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances following a CPIAUCSL jump above 0.5%, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) and nonfarm payrolls expansion (PAYEMS) confirms a self-reinforcing demand cycle that sustains corporate revenue growth, predicting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect disappears in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) provides a leading indicator for near-term corporate revenue trends, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth months consistently yield negative or flat 5-day SPX returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1.4 million units alongside a stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5 percent signals peak consumer confidence and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the signal's performance degrades significantly when tested against a Fed tightening regime.
